statement.p: A child is stacking up tiles. Each tiles height is {{\frac{2}{3}}} of a centimeter. How many layers of tiles are needed to reach {12} centimeters in total height?
To reach the total height of {12} centimeters, layers of tiles are needed.

solution.p: In this problem, each layer of tile is {{\frac{2}{3}}} centimeters tall. The child will keep stacking up layers until the total height reaches {12} centimeters. This is like asking how many {{\frac{2}{3}}} centimeters are there in {12} centimeters, a division problem.
To reach the total height of {12} centimeters, {18} layers of tiles are needed.
